content: Heads up note from @Aakash 2025-04-04T19:20:00.000+05:30: Sharing a brief note for our $50K investment in Ask My Guru’s pre-seed round, amounting to 0.6% ownership. This is a community cheque underwriting with Abhishek Goyal backing the co The co is raising $1.5 Mn at $8 M post money. Round is being led by Lumikai with participation from MPL This was brought to us by Sai at MPL and product was strongly vouched for with a strong reference on Krishna. We are restricting this to a community cheque given this is pre-traction. The founding team has strong FMF and knows the consumer tech growth playbook well. They are taking an AI first approach and launching first with astrology. About Co - Ask My Guru is building AI + human (expert) in the loep astrology platform - They are doing deep work on data set gathering around vedic shastras to unlock conversational AI for vedic astrology, vaastu and ayurveda and more - Building the platform in a co-bot approach of gurus (seasoned vedic experts) with their AI avatars Founding Team 1. Krishna Mohan 1. Vivek Sadamate - SVP Design at MPL - NID Ahmedabad grad Founder BRCs: The founders had strong soft refs from MPL (Sai, Shashank and  Shubh) : MPL is investing $300K from the balance sheet. Deeper BRCs we are running now as we form a view for a follow on investment Considerations - Experience operators with strong FMF and past experience of building and scaling consumer tech product - Unique differentiated arpproach to build for a large TAM Ri...

Key Qs: 1. Meesho learnings 1. What was the fist company - Worlzen - learnings? 1. What was 2nd company - Rotee - learnings? 1. How did LT happen? 1. Learnings from Valmo? 1. Learnings from Meesho Grocery? 1. Current state 1. Why Parag? 1. Why do concierge? 1. What is the wedge? 1. How to solve long tail supply? 1. What is the differentiation in the approach? 1. ICP 1. Monetization
RM reflections - Maybe: ++ Structured - clarity on NSM - drilled in from Meesho ++ Product mind ++ Reasonably outside in - studied Crew, interviewed Faff users ++ Highest level of FMF for orchestration play given Valmo +? Interesting wedge - non-knowledge work | outside the house tasks | chottu is the gig worker +? Some exposure to managing offline field Ops but should probe L1 | Built Valmo partner app & onboarded 2L+ partners | Although I don’t think these guys can do it outside Meesho +? When probed, very sharp learnings on founder journey in context of Meesho ?? Wants to do Ops light / orchestration - digitization of supply v/s owning supply || Feel pattern match from Meesho (8.5 Lakh sellers online) || Supply backwards approach ?? Commercial skills remain a question mark | “5% of supply charges” What would change my views: - Reflect on this approach - V/s other founders we have met? - Quality of L1s to manage Ops
RM notes: - Lucknow born - BTech Marine BITS 2014 - Raising $2M to $3M - $500K commitment - Vidit participation convo TBD Cash POV - This is Task Rabbit like play from USA

Company Snapshot: Problem & solution Current tax filing journey is friction-prone, manual, and error‑prone with late submissions and notices. AXO proposes proactive tax planning, integrated filing, and year‑round wealth optimisation, using automated data ingestion from AIS/Accounts Aggregator (AA)/eRI, continuous monitoring, and AI‑driven planning to turn tax compliance into a wealth management asset. Ideal customer profile and pricing Target segments and personas Early professional: simple filing; basic 80C deductions; MF/NPS suggestions; first‑time tax education. Mid‑career professional: regime selection; HRA/LTA, 80C/D; MF SIPs, NPS for tax saving; proactive planning. Senior professional: capital gains, ESOP/RSU, property income; quarterly advance tax; active portfolio management. Pricing model (current players’ bands) Low complexity (Salary/Single Form 16): ₹0–₹799 Mid complexity (F&O traders/crypto/freelancers): ₹2,500–₹4,500 High complexity (NRIs/foreign assets/audits): ₹7,500–₹15,000+ [Inference] Mass‑affluent segment targeted; ARPU uplift expected with wealth products and year‑round planning. Status of product & traction highlight Roadmap focuses on early adoption and scaling milestones; aims to reach significant user bases with annual targets: Target: 20k users by AY 2026‑27 Target: 100k users by AY 2027‑28 Target: 400k users by AY 2028‑29 Target: 800k users by AY 2029‑30 Funding request: ~$3 million to launch, iterate, and reach Series A readiness [Data source: Road...

The ICICI Bank contract itself can be $1M 1. Possible to charge both the issuer and the acquiring Bank for the same service 1. Notes:: BillMe acquisition • BillMe serves as a product hook o Price point is not a factor o Net revenue isn't the focus here • Current acquisition rate: 2K stores monthly o 80% from new enterprise sales (not MSMEs) o 99% through cross-selling by other teams • Acquisition completed in September 2023 o Initially structured as Cash + Equity o Converted to all-cash deal due to FOCC restrictions • Targets by Sept 2025 o 10x growth in stores and transactions o Systems implementation complete New venture (initial concept) • Consumer focus o Premium artisanal cookie QSR o High-end positioning o Social media-driven marketing o Aiming to be India's Krumble o Future expansion into other products • Break-even at 40 orders daily o No on-site cooking required • Store network collaboration • BillMe integration advantages o Access to target group data and locations o Cookie data cross-selling potential with McD • Target audience: Social media-savvy women (Instagram-focused) • Financial metrics o INR 350 Average Order Value o INR 200 Average Selling Price • Product specifications o Premium quality cookies o Dense, cheesecake-like texture o 2-3 day shelf life • Launch location: Bangalore o Tech-savvy population o Affordable rent (INR 250/sqft) o Mumbai launch later when brand is established • Projected financials o Monthly revenue: INR 8-12L o Daily target: 100 orders × INR 350 = INR 10L monthly

Notes from convo with @Anonymous, @Anonymous + Z47 Consumer: My take on him - spikey 0.5-1 on hustle and picking business getting to some answers that work - ⁠however very low on consumer insights - ⁠has very low commercial depth. He had thought through most of our questions things at a high level but lacked depth in almost everything. - ⁠maybe+ overall as a guy to back but we will need to fund his journey to maturity and depth. Will burn through a lot of money - Family background - Business model and thinking has evolved a lot Mohit: Spikey 1 + Spike is clarity of thought CV: Spikey 1 + Had a lot of depth for a guy not from the industry ? Will blow through money because he's naïve DC ? Doubts on his execution skills Kishan: Spikey 0.5 -1 DG: Maybe +/SM for DeVC | Maybe - for Z47 (want to track for next round)Spikey 0.5 (tending to 1) + Very first principled and strategic - one of the sharpest articulation on vertical QC + High IQ guy with a clear chip on the shoulder + Entrepreneurial - has had a history of always wanting to start up + Real H&H - very rare to find entrepreneurs this young doing this grit (likely well off also - so pure ambition) + Structured - everything had an excel/notion + analytical + ballsy guy - openly asked if we are just collecting data ? What is the execution spike - a lot of it is just thought right now - why hasn’t he added more brands? ? Does he run from trend to trend? Started from Blue-collar to Bookmarks to QC ? No history of achievement - if ...
